value.reason: The cited page explicitly documents "Thread automations" as a distinct feature within OpenAI's Codex Automations system. The page defines them as "heartbeat-style recurring wake-up calls attached to the current thread," used "when you want Codex to keep returning to the same conversation on a schedule" and when "the scheduled work should preserve the thread's context instead of starting from a new prompt each time." This directly matches both the cell label ("Thread Automations") and the cell description (scheduling/triggering recurring agent tasks tied to a specific conversation thread, running automatically without user supervision, configured within an existing thread). The page also clearly distinguishes thread automations from standalone/project automations, confirming this is a specific named sub-feature. The description's claims are all faithful to the page with no invented content.
